series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 14 years
comments:
The State Auditor’s Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas state agencies.

series_title:
Biennial Budget Requests /
Legislative Appropriation
Requests (LARs)
series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the requests to
the University Archivist. Only copies
of supporting documentation
submitted to the Legislative Budget
Board are archival.

series_description:
From employees and others concerning
the institution and records pertaining to
the resolution of the complaint.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item
number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence/email, in any media,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an institution.
comments:
SEE comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.

Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an institution.

Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records, purchased with state funds or
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
University Archivist when these
records have met their retention
periods. CAUTION: A record of this
type purchased with personal
funds, but used by a state official to
document his or her work activities
may be a state record and subject
to this retention period. See Open
Records Decision 635 issued in
December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, electronic, audio,
and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are exempt under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services or projects of an institution that
are not included in or directly related to
other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description: Reports, Studies & Analyses
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_title:
Long- and Short-Range
Data Processing Plans
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meeting notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Reports, Studies, and
Surveys - Final (non-
routine or special) /
Customer Surveys
series_description:
Surveys returned by the customer or
clients of an agency, and the statistical
data maintained rating an agency’s
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
SEE item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers or
presentations delivered in conjunction
with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Suggestions submitted by institution
personnel and responses.

Instructional materials developed by an
institution for training entities or
individuals it regulates or serves.

Records created by or on behalf of an
institution in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit. (Also includes
files/records related to formal complaints
filed with the Equal Employment
Opportunity Commission (EEOC) of the
U.S. Department of Labor.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
All statutes of limitations must have
been met before file is closed.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
University Archivist for archival
preservation.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies
as required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed
with the Texas Ethics Commission
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Includes information resources and
operational strategic plans prepared in
accordance with Tex. Govt. Code, §§
2054.095 and 2056.002.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
UTSA retains the permanent
Record Copy and sends required
copies to the University Archivist.

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with Americans With
Disabilities Act.

Official agenda and minutes of state
boards/committees, commissions, and
councils that conduct open meetings as
required by Government Code, Chapter
551.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTES: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the University
Archivist. CAUTION: This records
series and item number 1.1.059,
1.1.060, 1.1.061 and 1.1.062 must
be used for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_title:
Meetings, Certified
Agendas or Tape
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Certified agendas or tape recordings of
closed meetings of state boards,
commissions, committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_title:
Meetings, Audio or
Videotapes of Open
series_description:
Audio or videotapes of open meetings of
state boards, commissions, committees,
etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
CAUTION: Minutes of state
agencies are permanent records.
Audio and videotapes are not
permanent media. State agencies
may not retain audio or videotape
of the meetings of governing
bodies in lieu of written minutes.
The proceedings of all meetings of
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils must be
reduced to writing. SEE caution
comment at item number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an actual
meeting.
comments:
SEE caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal staff
meetings.

Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency’s appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
comments:
CAUTION: The FE+3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records
series in this schedule if the records
series is needed for documentation
of agency performance measures.

series_title:
Reports, Studies, and
Surveys - Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing
non-fiscal reports.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 1.1.064.

series_title:
Reports - Annual and
Biennial Agency - Record
Copy
series_description:
Biennial and Annual narrative reports to
the governor and legislature as required.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of the reports to
the University Archivist.

series_title:
Reports - Administrative
or By Consultants or
Committees (not including
fiscal records)
series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency’s programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or by consultants under
contract with an agency that are not
noted elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
Energy Management Reports and other
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.

Quarterly and annual reports on
institution performance measures
submitted to the executive and legislative
offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
UTSA retains the permanent
Record Copy.

series_title:
Reports - Employee
(activity and production
type reports)
series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining
to workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
comments: CAUTION: See item number 1.1.064

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
pubic or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services or projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments: SEE ALSO: Item number 1.1.071

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies, and
Procedures - Working Files
series_description:
Working files used in the development of
manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally for the
public or for those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
and agencies and programs, services or
projects
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments: SEE ALSO: item number 1.1.070

series_description:
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes. The records may be
maintained with related information
including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code Chapter 2009.054(c), associated
with a matter conducted under an
alternative dispute resolution procedure in
which personnel of a state agency
participated as a party on the agency's
behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071.

series_title:
Deeds and Other Legal
Documents Reflecting
Legal Title
series_description:
Property deeds, appraisals, surveys,
description of property and all other
supporting documents.
comments:
Note: documents of ownership of
vehicles are retained per 5.6.007.

series_description: Not related to litigation
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Note: If correspondence relates to
a matter in litigation, see 1.1.048.

series_description:
Any documents relating to a patent or
trademark.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years

series_description:
Any documents relating to a copyright
(whether officially registered with the US
Patent and Trademark Office or not).
Possible claim under Texas Tort Claims Act
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: If matter leads to litigation
see 1.1.048.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Grants and sponsors may
have individuals guidelines for
retention. Records must be
retained in accordance with
guidelines of grant or contract.

series_description:
Legal documents requiring recipient to
appear in court to testify, or to produce
records to be used in litigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Executed (signed) release/indemnity
forms.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Agency level documents authorizing final
disposition of records under a certified
records retention schedule.

Print masters of original version and all
subsequent revisions to an institution
form, including any associated design or
design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Any periodic listing of all forms used
internally or externally by an agency.

UTSA copy of SLR 105. Includes
documentation of certification and
approval - forms SLR 105C, and/or other
forms designated by the State Records
Administrator.
comments:
Original is retained by Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms indicate records transferred to
storage or a transfer of legal custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Request for Authority to
Dispose of State Records
(RMD 102)
series_description: UTSA Copy
comments:
Original is retained by SLRMD of
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission.

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
destroyed or transferred to the University
Archivist, showing records series title,
dates of records, and date destroyed or
transferred.
UTSA copy.

Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists,
registers, guides, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.

series_title:
Institution Publications -
Record Copy (Not
University Catalogs)
series_description:
One copy of each institution publication
except a publication that is subject to a
different retention period in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
Government Code 441.101-
441.106). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to periodic
evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further
retention. SEE 6.4.108 for
University Catalogs.

series_description:
Background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives, prints,
flats, etc. This includes all work
performed both inside and outside the
institution.

comments:
May be transferred to the
University Archivist after 10 years
but must be maintained
permanently.

series_title:
Completed Class Tests and
Examinations, Students’
Course Papers
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Coursework under
dispute may not be destroyed until
the resolution of the dispute.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Curriculum Files, Including
Revisions, Central
Administrative
comments:
Review before disposal as some
may merit permanent retention for
historical reasons.

Review before disposal as some
may merit permanent retention for
historical reasons.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Grade sheets submitted by instructors,
advanced credit posting authorizations,
and grade rolls or similar input
documentation used in posting grades or
credit data to transcripts
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This series documents students'
evaluations of non-tenure track teaching
personnel and is used to help determine
faculty promotion, merit increases and/or
to review instructional courses and
programs. These records provide
students' opinions on faculty members'
familiarity with current literature of the
discipline, preparation, assignments,
examinations, lecture styles, willingness to
engage in dialogue, and availability.
Records include: bubble forms (input
documents); course reaction; inventory
printouts; statistical tabulation; summary
reports; and related documentation and
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This series documents students'
evaluations of tenure track teaching
personnel and is used to help determine
faculty promotion, merit increases and/or
to review instructional courses and
programs. These records provide
students' opinions on faculty members'
familiarity with current literature of the
discipline, preparation, assignments,
examinations, lecture styles, willingness to
engage in dialogue, and availability.
Records include: bubble forms (input
documents); course reaction; inventory
printouts; statistical tabulation; summary
reports; and related documentation and
correspondence.

series_title:
Leave Forms and Travel
Files, Faculty

New Course Proposals
File, Central
Administrative
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: Review before disposal as
some may merit permanent
retention for historical reasons.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Note: Review before disposal as
some may merit permanent
retention for historical reasons.

series_title:
Software Programs and
Job Control Language
series_description:
Automated software applications and
operating system files including job
control language, program listing/source
code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, and equipment
control systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records and their
timely, authorized disposition including
documentation describing how a system
operates and necessary for using the
system such as user guides, system or sub-
system definitions, system specifications,
input and output specifications, and
system flow charts; program descriptions
and documentation such as program
flowcharts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, and other records that
document modifications to computer
programs; and data documentation
necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate
and interpret data in an automated
system such as a data element dictionary,
file layout, code book or table, and other
records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits
such as those showing transactions
accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or
processed; history files/tapes; records of
on-line updates to application files or
security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Finding Aids, Indexes and
Tracking Systems
series_description:
Automated indexes, lists, registers and
other finding aids used to provide access
to the hard copy and electronic records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
Electronic files or automated logs created
to monitor computer systems such as
print spool logs, tape activity logs, etc.

series_title:
Chargeback Records to
Data Processing Services
Users
series_description:
Records used to document, calculate
costs, and bill program units for computer
usage and data processing services. These
records are also used for cost recovery,
budgeting, or administrative purposes.

Schedules or similar records showing
computer jobs to be run and other reports
by computer operators or programmers of
work performed.
comments:
Note: These may be kept longer if
they possess additional
administrative value.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to reconcile batches
submitted for processing against batches
received and processed.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Output Records for
Computer Production
(Mini and Mainframe)
series_description:
Reports showing transactions that were
accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or
processed.
comments:
Output may be either in printed or
magnetic form. If magnetic, the
files, programs and documentation
must be available.

series_description:
I nformation verifying the quality of
system, hardware or software operations
including records of errors or failures and
the loss of data resulting from such
failures, documentation of abnormal
termination and of error free processing,
checks of changes put into production,
transaction histories, and other records
needed as an audit trail to evaluate data
accuracy.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Data resident on hard drives that make
use of user-specific information
transmitted by the Web server onto the
user’s computer so that the information
might be available for later access by itself
or other servers.
comments:
The disposal of Internet cookies
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(1.2.001) or in records disposition
logs (1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_description:
A record of the documents visited during
an Internet session that allows users to
access previously visited pages more
quickly or to generate a record of usage of
a state-owned computer.
comments:
The disposal of History Files need
not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(1.2.001) or in records disposition
logs (1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(1.2.014).

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreements

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
legal_citation:
41 CFR 60-300.44
and 41 CFR 60-
741.44 (Federal
Affirmative Action,
Veterans); 29 CFR
1602.31 (State
Agencies); 29 CFR
1602.49(a) (State
Universities)

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Copies of information relating to the
selection by employees of life, disability,
health, and other types of insurance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorization
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes notes of interviews with
candidates, questions asked of applicants,
audio and videotapes of job interviews,
driving records, and previous injury
checks, pre-employment physical
examinations, pre-employment drug
testing, polygraph examination results;
and all other records that document the
selection process.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
criminal history checks. SEE item
number 3.1.026.
legal_citation:
41 CFR 60-300.44
and 41 CFR 60-
741.44 (Federal
Affirmative Action,
Veterans); 29 CFR
1602.31(State
Agencies), 29 CFR
1602.49(a) and Texas
Government Code
§411.094(e) (State
Universities)

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an employee
with the Equal Employment
Opportunity Commission (EEOC) of
the U.S. Department of Labor. See
Item Number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Position/Job Descriptions
(for individuals; classified
and nonclassified
positions)
series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Medical or physical examination reports or
certificates of employees for whom
periodic monitoring of health or fitness is
required. (not to include pre-employment
physicals)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include pre-
employment physical examinations.
SEE item 3.1.014.

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is

period.
